subtitle: Inner thigh strain — less common but slow to heal

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Adductor strain more common in trail runners and those doing lateral drills

mild.description: Inner thigh tightness or ache, particularly on uphill or wide stride
mild.can_run_note: Yes — avoid wide stride and lateral movements
why: Adductor strain from lateral movement, wide stride, or trail running
Weak adductors unable to cope with running volume
Hip flexor tightness altering pelvis position and increasing adductor load
relief: Butterfly stretch sitting — soles together, lean forward gently 60s
Standing adductor stretch — wide stance, shift weight side to side
prevention: Avoid sudden lateral movement without warm-up
Pelvic stability work to reduce adductor compensatory load

moderate.description: Groin pain during running, especially push-off phase
moderate.can_run_note: Short flat runs only — stop if groin pain increases
why: Adductor longus strain at or near its pubic attachment
Athletic pubalgia — chronic groin pain from pelvic instability
Hip joint pathology referring into the groin
relief: Isometric adductor squeeze with pillow between knees 45s × 5
Reduce stride width and avoid cambered roads
prevention: Gradual return to trail running or lateral work

severe.description: Acute groin pain, swelling, cannot adduct leg against resistance
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — adductor injuries can become chronic if rushed
why: Grade 2 or 3 adductor strain requiring imaging
Pubic symphysis stress reaction in high mileage runners
Hip joint issue requiring differential diagnosis
relief: Complete rest from running and lower body loading
Physiotherapy assessment — differentiate from hip joint pathology
Imaging to grade injury and rule out pubic stress reaction
prevention: Full rehabilitation before return — adductor injuries recur if rushed
Copenhagen plank and single-leg stability as ongoing maintenance

see_physio_if: Groin pain at rest not linked to activity
No improvement after 1 week of rest
